heading: Susceptibility test interpretive criteria for microorganisms
label: 360a–2 Susceptibility test interpretive criteria for microorganisms

children.label: A
children.text: efficiently update susceptibility test interpretive criteria for antimicrobial drugs when necessary for public health, due to, among other things, the constant evolution of microorganisms that leads to the development of resistance to drugs that have been effective in decreasing morbidity and mortality for patients, which warrants unique management of antimicrobial drugs that is inappropriate for most other drugs in order to delay or prevent the development of further resistance to existing therapies;

children.label: B
children.text: provide for public notice of the availability of recognized interpretive criteria and interpretive criteria standards; and

children.label: C
children.text: clear under , classify under , or approve under , antimicrobial susceptibility testing devices utilizing updated, recognized susceptibility test interpretive criteria to characterize the in vitro susceptibility of particular bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable, to antimicrobial drugs.section 360(k) of this titlesection 360c(f)(2) of this titlesection 360e of this title

children.label: A
children.text: if such criteria are available on the date of approval of the drug under or licensure of the drug under (as applicable), upon such approval or licensure; orsection 355 of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.label: B
children.text: if such criteria are unavailable on such date, on the date on which such criteria are available for such drug.

children.label: A
children.text: preclinical and clinical data, including pharmacokinetic, pharmacodynamic, and epidemiological data;

children.label: B
children.text: the relationship of susceptibility test interpretive criteria to morbidity and mortality associated with the disease or condition for which such drug is used; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other evidence and information as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.heading: Susceptibility test Interpretive Criteria Website
children.label: b Susceptibility test Interpretive Criteria Website

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall establish, and maintain thereafter, on the website of the Food and Drug Administration, a dedicated website that contains a list of any appropriate new or updated susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards and interpretive criteria in accordance with paragraph (2) (referred to in this section as the “Interpretive Criteria Website”).December 13, 2016

children.heading: Listing of susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards and interpretive criteria
children.label: 2 Listing of susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards and interpretive criteria
I
children.text: establishes and maintains procedures to address potential conflicts of interest and ensure transparent decisionmaking;

children.label: II
children.text: holds open meetings to ensure that there is an opportunity for public input by interested parties, and establishes and maintains processes to ensure that such input is considered in decisionmaking; and

children.label: III
children.text: permits its standards to be made publicly available, through the National Library of Medicine or another similar source acceptable to the Secretary; and

children.label: ii
children.text: recognized in whole, or in part, by the Secretary under subsection (c).

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary does not recognize, in whole or in part, an interpretive criteria standard described under subparagraph (A) otherwise applicable to such a drug;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary withdraws under subsection (c)(1)(A) recognition of a standard, in whole or in part, otherwise applicable to such a drug;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Secretary approves an application under or , as applicable, with respect to marketing of such a drug for which there are no relevant interpretive criteria included in a standard recognized by the Secretary under subsection (c); orsection 355 of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.label: I
children.text: differ from otherwise applicable interpretive criteria included in a standard listed under subparagraph (A) or interpretive criteria otherwise listed under this subparagraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: are determined by the Secretary to be appropriate for the drug.

children.label: i
children.text: that the website provides information about the in vitro susceptibility of bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable to a certain drug (or drugs);

children.label: I
children.text: the safety and efficacy of such drugs in treating clinical infections due to such bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable, may or may not have been established in adequate and well-controlled clinical trials in order for the susceptibility information described in clause (i) to be included on the website; and

children.label: II
children.text: the clinical significance of such susceptibility information in such instances is unknown;

children.label: iii
children.text: that the approved product labeling for specific drugs provides the uses for which the Secretary has approved the product; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information that the Secretary determines appropriate to adequately convey the meaning of the data supporting the recognition or listing of susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards or susceptibility test interpretive criteria included on the website.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 3 Notice
children.text: Not later than the date on which the Interpretive Criteria Website is established, the Secretary shall publish a notice of that establishment in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Inapplicability of misbranding provision
children.label: 4 Inapplicability of misbranding provision
children.text: The inclusion in the approved labeling of an antimicrobial drug of a reference or hyperlink to the Interpretive Criteria Website, in and of itself, shall not cause the drug to be misbranded in violation of .section 352 of this title

children.heading: Trade secrets and confidential information
children.label: 5 Trade secrets and confidential information
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as authorizing the Secretary to disclose any information that is a trade secret or confidential information subject to .section 552(b)(4) of title 5

children.heading: Recognition of susceptibility test interpretive criteria
children.label: c Recognition of susceptibility test interpretive criteria
i
children.text: evaluate any appropriate new or updated susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards established by a nationally or internationally recognized standard development organization described in subsection (b)(2)(A)(i); and

children.label: I
children.text: withdrawing recognition of any different susceptibility test interpretive criteria standard, in whole or in part;

children.label: II
children.text: recognizing the new or updated standards;

children.label: III
children.text: recognizing one or more parts of the new or updated interpretive criteria specified in such a standard and declining to recognize the remainder of such standard; and

children.label: IV
children.text: making any necessary updates to the lists under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Upon approval of a drug
children.label: B Upon approval of a drug
children.text: Upon the approval of an initial or supplemental application for an antimicrobial drug under or , as applicable, where such approval is based on susceptibility test interpretive criteria which differ from those contained in a standard recognized, or from those otherwise listed, by the Secretary pursuant to this subsection, or for which there are no relevant interpretive criteria standards recognized, or interpretive criteria otherwise listed, by the Secretary pursuant to this subsection, the Secretary shall update the lists under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (b)(2) to include the susceptibility test interpretive criteria upon which such approval was based.section 355 of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.heading: Bases for updating interpretive criteria standards
children.label: 2 Bases for updating interpretive criteria standards
A
children.text: the Secretary’s determination that such a standard is not applicable to a particular drug because the characteristics of the drug differ from other drugs with the same active ingredient;

children.label: B
children.text: information provided by interested third parties, including public comment on the annual compilation of notices published under paragraph (3);

children.label: C
children.text: any bases used to identify susceptibility test interpretive criteria under subsection (a)(2); and

children.label: D
children.text: such other information or factors as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: recognized by the Secretary under this subsection; or

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise listed on the Interpretive Criteria Website under subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Relation tosection 360d(c) of this title
children.label: 4 Relation tosection 360d(c) of this title
children.text: Any susceptibility test interpretive standard recognized under this subsection or any criteria otherwise listed under subsection (b)(2)(B) shall be deemed to be recognized as a standard by the Secretary under .section 360d(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Voluntary use of interpretive criteria
children.label: 5 Voluntary use of interpretive criteria
children.text: Nothing in this section prohibits a person from seeking approval or clearance of a drug or device, or changes to the drug or the device, on the basis of susceptibility test interpretive criteria which differ from those contained in a standard recognized, or from those otherwise listed, by the Secretary pursuant to subsection (b)(2).

children.heading: Drugs marketed prior to establishment of Interpretive Criteria Website
children.label: 1 Drugs marketed prior to establishment of Interpretive Criteria Website

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to an antimicrobial drug lawfully introduced or delivered for introduction into interstate commerce for commercial distribution before the establishment of the Interpretive Criteria Website, a holder of an approved application under or , as applicable, for each such drug, not later than 1 year after establishment of the Interpretive Criteria Website described in subsection (b)(1), shall remove susceptibility test interpretive criteria, if any, and related information from the approved drug labeling and replace it with a reference to the Interpretive Criteria Website.section 355 of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.heading: Labeling changes
children.label: B Labeling changes
children.text: The labeling changes required by this section shall be considered a minor change under section 314.70 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations) that may be implemented through documentation in the next applicable annual report.

children.heading: Drugs marketed subsequent to establishment of Interpretive Criteria Website
children.label: 2 Drugs marketed subsequent to establishment of Interpretive Criteria Website
children.text: With respect to antimicrobial drugs approved on or after the date of the establishment of the Interpretive Criteria Website described in subsection (b)(1), the labeling for such a drug shall include, in lieu of susceptibility test interpretive criteria and related information, a reference to such Website.

children.heading: Special condition for marketing of antimicrobial susceptibility testing devices
children.label: e Special condition for marketing of antimicrobial susceptibility testing devices

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding sections 351, 352, 355, 360, 360c, and 360e of this title, if the conditions specified in paragraph (2) are met (in addition to other applicable provisions under this subchapter) with respect to an antimicrobial susceptibility testing device described in subsection (f)(1), the Secretary may authorize the marketing of such device for a use described in such subsection.

children.heading: Conditions applicable to antimicrobial susceptibility testing devices
children.label: 2 Conditions applicable to antimicrobial susceptibility testing devices
i
children.text: included in a standard recognized by the Secretary under subsection (c); or

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise listed on the Interpretive Criteria Website under subsection (b)(2).

children.label: i
children.text: that the device provides information about the in vitro susceptibility of bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable to antimicrobial drugs;

children.label: I
children.text: the safety and efficacy of such drugs in treating clinical infections due to such bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable, may or may not have been established in adequate and well-controlled clinical trials in order for the device to report the susceptibility of such bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable, to such drugs; and

children.label: II
children.text: the clinical significance of such susceptibility information in those instances is unknown;

children.label: iii
children.text: that the approved labeling for drugs tested using such a device provides the uses for which the Secretary has approved such drugs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information the Secretary determines appropriate to adequately convey the meaning of the data supporting the recognition or listing of susceptibility test interpretive criteria standards or susceptibility test interpretive criteria described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: C
children.text: The antimicrobial susceptibility testing device meets all other requirements to be cleared under , classified under , or approved under .section 360(k) of this titlesection 360c(f)(2) of this titlesection 360e of this title

The term “antimicrobial susceptibility testing device” means a device that utilizes susceptibility test interpretive criteria to determine and report the in vitro susceptibility of certain microorganisms to a drug (or drugs).
The term “qualified infectious disease product” means a qualified infectious disease product designated under .section 355f(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: one or more specific numerical values which characterize the susceptibility of bacteria or other microorganisms to the drug tested; and

children.label: B
children.text: related categorizations of such susceptibility, including categorization of the drug as susceptible, intermediate, resistant, or such other term as the Secretary determines appropriate.

children.label: i
children.text: is intended for human use in the treatment of a disease or condition caused by a bacterium or fungus;

children.label: ii
children.text: may include a qualified infectious disease product designated under ; andsection 355f(d) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: is subject to .section 353(b)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: drugs other than systemic antibacterial and antifungal drugs; and

children.label: ii
children.text: biological products (as such term is defined in ) to the extent such products exhibit antimicrobial activity.section 262 of title 42

The term “interpretive criteria standard” means a compilation of susceptibility test interpretive criteria developed by a standard development organization that meets the criteria set forth in subsection (b)(2)(A)(i).

alter the standards of evidence under subsection (c) or (d) of (including the substantial evidence standard under ) or under (as applicable); orsection 355 of this titlesection 355(d) of this titlesection 262 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: apply with respect to any drug, device, or biological product, in any context other than an antimicrobial drug and an antimicrobial susceptibility testing device that uses susceptibility test interpretive criteria to characterize and report the susceptibility of certain bacteria, fungi, or other microorganisms, as applicable, to such drug to reflect patient morbidity and mortality in accordance with this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: unless specifically stated, have any effect on authorities provided under other sections of this chapter, including any regulations issued under such sections.
